methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Quantum Brilliance is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and others operating in quantum computing infrastructure and applications.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised ($50M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B to subsequent-stage progression, the analysis yields an implied valuation of $95M. The Peer-Median method was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer datapoints at the same or adjacent stage within similar valuation tiers; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none of the filtered subsets met the minimum threshold of 3 comparable companies needed for reliable median benchmarking.

The triangulated valuation range is $81M to $109M, with a median of $95M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries meaningful uncertainty around stage-progression assumptions and the lack of direct peer validation. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for this particular quantum subsector, the timing and terms of prior fundraising rounds, and whether comparable firms represent true operational parity or stage equivalence to Quantum Brilliance.
